title: Pentacalia ricoensis
intro: Pentacalia ricoensis is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to Hawaii. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia ricoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Pentacalia ricoensis is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
taxonomy: Pentacalia ricoensis is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family consists of over 23,000 species and is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Pentacalia ricoensis is further classified in the genus Pentacalia, which consists of over 150 species of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The genus Pentacalia is a member of the tribe Senecioneae, within the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification helps to group and identify Pentacalia ricoensis within the larger plant kingdom.
